info.title: whisper-wrap engine API
info.description: HTTP/WebSocket/SSE API for the whisper-wrap transcription engine.

200.description: API discovery document — a hand-maintained list of top-level routes. Token-exempt.
The prompt-action registry (categories + actions).

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-call model override (llm-provider-abstraction). Absent
selects the active provider's default model.

requestBody.description: Either a JSON body `{"text": "…"}` for a text question, or raw/multipart audio (`audio/*`, `application/octet-stream`, or `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part) to transcribe-then-ask.

200.description: Answer. When `stream=true` the response is a `text/event-stream` (SSE) of incremental `data:` chunks terminated by a final event; otherwise a single JSON answer object.

503.description: No ASR model loaded (audio input) or no LLM provider configured.

200.description: Auxiliary (diarization + VAD) models with install state.

post.summary:
Start (or report an already-running) download. Returns immediately; poll
GET /aux-models/download/{id}.

requestBody.description: The auxiliary model id to download.

200.description: Download started/queued, or already present. `status = "done"` adds `already_present: true`; `status = "downloading"` omits it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Auxiliary model id whose download progress to read.

200.description: Current download progress: an `installed` form (`status` "done"/"idle" + `installed`) or a `progress` form (byte counters).

delete.summary:
Cancel an in-flight aux download (mirror of models::cancel_download, keyed
by aux id). The worker notices the flag between chunks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Auxiliary model id whose download to cancel.

200.description: Download cancellation acknowledged.

404.description: No active download for that auxiliary model.

delete.summary: Uninstall an aux model (delete its ONNX file from disk).

200.description: Auxiliary model weights removed.

get.summary: `GET /config/ai` — masked read of the active config.

200.description: The masked, read-safe AI provider config.

put.summary:
`PUT /config/ai` — validate, save, swap the live client, return the masked
view. Invalid provider -> 400. Empty `apiKey` keeps the stored key.

requestBody.description: Partial AI provider config update (camelCase). Unknown keys are ignored; the runtime accepts a lenient JSON object.

200.description: The updated masked config.

get.summary:
`GET /config/ai/models` — list provider models using the SUBMITTED
provider/base-url/key. Any fetch failure -> 200 with `models: []` + a
non-null `error` (never a 5xx).

200.description: Available models for the given provider/baseUrl/apiKey, or an error descriptor embedded in the JSON.

post.summary:
`POST /config/ai/test` — build a transient client from the submitted body,
do one minimal non-streaming `ask`, report `{ ok, error }`. Never persists.

200.description: Connectivity test result `{ok, error}` (failures are reported in the body, not as a non-200 status).

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/ai?model=` — run a prompt over the item's transcript (D5).
Hard DAG gate: the item must exist (404) and have a transcript (409); the
LLM must be configured (503). `?model=` is recorded but does not switch the
LLM until llm-provider-abstraction.

requestBody.description: The prompt to run against the item's transcript.

409.description: The item has no transcript yet, or an AI run is already in progress.

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/diarize?quality=` — diarize the item's stored audio (D4).
When the item has a transcript the diarization is merged into
speaker-attributed segments; otherwise the raw speaker turns are recorded.

404.description: No item or stored audio for that id.

409.description: A diarization run is already in progress for the item.

get.summary:
`GET /items/{id}/runs` — every run recorded against an item (oldest first),
each with its job-status contract + result snapshot. The run inspector
(fe-item-detail-runs) reads this to show the re-runnable pipeline history.
An item with no runs returns an empty list (not a 404).

200.description: All runs for the item (oldest first) as `{ "runs": RunRecord[] }`; an empty list when the item has no runs.

post.summary:
`POST /items/{id}/transcribe?model=` — transcribe the item's stored audio on
the chosen model, snapshotting the transcript into a transcribe run (D3).

202.description: Transcription run accepted — returns the run descriptor; poll `GET /runs/{id}`.

404.description: No item or stored audio for that id.

409.description: A transcription run is already in progress for the item.

get.description: WebSocket endpoint for live captioning. The client performs a WebSocket upgrade, then streams 16 kHz mono `pcm_s16le` audio as binary frames. The server emits JSON text messages: `{"type":"partial"|"final","text","start_ms","end_ms"}`, `{"type":"warning","message":...}`, or `{"type":"error","message":...}` followed by close code 1003. OpenAPI 3.1 cannot model the bidirectional frame protocol, so this entry is descriptive only and declares no request or response body schema.

101.description: Switching Protocols — the connection is upgraded to WebSocket.

200.description: Installed + registered ASR models with active/loaded state.

requestBody.description: The model name to load as the active engine.

200.description: Model activated (or a no-op when already active + loaded).

post.summary:
Start (or report an already-running) download of a model's ggml
weights. Returns immediately; poll GET /models/download/{name}.

200.description: Download started/queued, or already present. `status = "done"` adds `already_present: true`; `status = "downloading"` omits it.

409.description: A download for this model is already in progress.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Model name whose download progress to read.

200.description: Current download progress: an `installed` form (`status` "done"/"idle" + `installed`) or a `progress` form (byte counters).

404.description: No active download for that model.

delete.summary:
Request cancellation of an in-flight download. The worker notices the
flag between chunks, removes the partial file, then flips the job to
"cancelled" — poll GET /models/download/{name} to observe it land.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Model name whose download to cancel.

200.description: Download cancellation acknowledged.

404.description: No active download for that model.

delete.summary:
Uninstall a model's on-disk weights (the "D" in model CRUD). Refuses to
remove the currently-loaded model — switch away first.

200.description: Model weights removed.

409.description: Model weights missing or cannot be removed.

get.summary:
`GET /runs/{id}` (D5): the job-status contract for any run, served from the
persisted row — so it answers correctly after a restart, when no in-memory
job remains. An unknown id is a 404 with the standard error envelope.

200.description: The run's job-status contract plus result snapshot.
Engine health + active-model snapshot. Token-exempt.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-request ASR model (per-request-asr-model). Absent selects
the active engine; present selects that model with no global swap.

requestBody.description: Audio payload — raw bytes with an `audio/*` (or `application/octet-stream`) Content-Type, or `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part.

200.description: Transcription result — text plus timing/metadata.

400.description: Empty or unreadable audio body, or missing multipart `file` field.

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Optional per-request ASR model (stage-run-endpoints D7). Absent selects
the active engine — the v2 behavior.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload with a `file` part carrying the meeting audio. Query params select the filename, diarization quality tier (`fast`|`balanced`), and optional per-request ASR model.

202.description: Job accepted — returns a job descriptor; poll `GET /transcribe/meeting/{id}` for progress.

400.description: Invalid quality tier or malformed upload (ad-hoc `{detail:{error,reason}}` body).

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Meeting job id returned by the submit call.

200.description: Current job status and, when finished, the result.

202.description: Cancellation requested for an in-flight job.

requestBody.description: OpenAI-compatible transcription request — `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part (audio) plus optional `model`/`language`/`response_format` fields.

200.description: Transcription result. The response shape varies by the `response_format` field: the default `json` returns `{"text": string}` (documented by the `OpenAiTranscription` schema below); `text`/`srt`/`vtt` return a `text/plain` (or `text/vtt`) body; `verbose_json` returns an extended JSON object with `segments`. The schema documents the default `json` form.

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

requestBody.description: OpenAI-compatible translation request (transcribe + translate to English) — `multipart/form-data` with a `file` part plus optional `model`/`response_format` fields.

200.description: English translation result. The response shape varies by the `response_format` field: the default `json` returns `{"text": string}` (documented by the `OpenAiTranscription` schema below); `text`/`srt`/`vtt` return a `text/plain` (or `text/vtt`) body; `verbose_json` returns an extended JSON object with `segments`. The schema documents the default `json` form.

413.description: Audio exceeds the configured maximum file size.

200.description: Paged meeting list with item-metadata filters applied.
The meeting with its items.
Meeting deleted.
Updated meeting.
The stored audio blob (binary, original media type).

404.description: No meeting or no stored audio for that id.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload carrying the meeting's audio blob in a `file` part.

200.description: Audio stored.
OpenAI-compatible model list `{object:"list", data:[…]}` describing the active ASR model.
Paged session list with item-metadata filters applied.
Cleared stored audio blobs across sessions.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of session-list changes. On connect the server emits a `ready` event, then a `changed` event whenever any session is created, updated, or deleted; comment heartbeats keep idle connections alive. Clients re-fetch `GET /v1/sessions` on each `changed`.

200.description: An SSE stream of `ready` then `changed` events.
The session with its items and finals.
Session deleted.
Updated session.
The stored audio blob (binary, original media type).

404.description: No session or no stored audio for that id.

requestBody.description: Multipart upload carrying the session's audio blob in a `file` part.

200.description: Audio stored.

requestBody.description: A finalized transcript segment to append to the session.

ActionsResponse.description:
Success body of `GET /actions` — the prompt-action registry.

`actions` and `categories` are kept as `Vec<serde_json::Value>` because
their element types (`whisper_wrap_core::actions::Action` / `Category`)
implement `Serialize` but not `ToSchema` and live in the core crate; typing
them here would require touching core, so the element shape is preserved
without over-typing.

AiConfigUpdate.description: Update body for `PUT /config/ai`. camelCase on the wire.

AiConfigView.description: The masked, read-safe view of the active config. Wire shape is camelCase.

AiTestResult.description:
Connectivity-test result for `POST /config/ai/test`. Wire shape is
`{ "ok": bool, "error": string | null }`: `error` is always present and is
`null` on success, so it is a plain `Option<String>` (emitted as `null`, not
omitted) to match today's `json!()` output byte-for-byte.

ApiErrorBody.description:
The `{ "detail": string }` body every [`ApiError`] serializes to. `ApiError`
itself is private and only implements `IntoResponse`, so it cannot derive
`ToSchema`; this is the single reusable schema every fallible operation
references for its non-200 error responses (rather than re-inlining the
shape per handler).

AskResponse.description:
Non-streaming answer body of `POST /ask` (`stream=false`). `transcript`
is the STT text for audio input and `null` for a text question — the key
is always present, never omitted. The streaming (`stream=true`) branch is
an SSE `text/event-stream` and is not described by this type.

answer.description: The LLM's answer to the question.

transcript.description: Transcript of the audio input, or `null` when the question was text.

AudioUploadResponse.description:
Shared body of `POST /v1/{sessions,meetings}/{id}/audio`: the stored blob's
path, size, and mime. Both upload handlers emit the same three keys.

AuxCancelDownloadResponse.description:
`DELETE /aux-models/download/{id}` acknowledgement: the aux id and the
resulting download `status` — `"cancelling"` when a live download was asked
to stop, otherwise the job's current (terminal) status.

AuxDeleteModelResponse.description:
`DELETE /aux-models/{id}` acknowledgement: the removed id and a constant
`removed: true` flag.

AuxDownloadResponse.description:
`POST /aux-models/download` success body. Mirrors `models::DownloadResponse`
but keyed by `id`: two states share the `id` + `status` discriminant — a
`status = "done"` response carries `already_present: true`, a
`status = "downloading"` response omits `already_present`.

AuxDownloadStatusResponse.description:
`GET /aux-models/download/{id}` progress body. Mirrors
`models::DownloadStatusResponse` but keyed by `id`: two disjoint wire shapes
→ an untagged enum (utoipa emits a `oneOf`) so `installed` never mixes with
the progress byte-counters.

- [`Installed`](Self::Installed) — weights on disk (`status = "done"`,
  `installed = true`) or nothing happening (`status = "idle"`,
  `installed = false`).
- [`Progress`](Self::Progress) — a job's byte counters; `total_bytes` and
  `error` are emitted as `null` when unknown (never omitted).

AuxListResponse.description:
`GET /aux-models` success body: the fixed auxiliary-model catalogue with
per-entry install state.

AuxModelEntry.description:
One row of `GET /aux-models` — a catalogue entry plus its on-disk install
state. Keys and types mirror the prior ad-hoc JSON exactly.

BulkClearAudioResponse.description: Body of `DELETE /v1/sessions/audio`: how many stored blobs were removed.

CancelDownloadResponse.description:
`DELETE /models/download/{name}` acknowledgement: the model name and the
resulting download `status` — `"cancelling"` when a live download was asked
to stop, otherwise the job's current (terminal) status.

job_id.description: Id of the job whose cancellation was requested.

DeleteModelResponse.description:
`DELETE /models/{name}` acknowledgement: the removed model name and a
constant `removed: true` flag.

DiscoveryResponse.description:
Success body of `GET /` — the hand-maintained API discovery document.

The wire shape is an object `{ "endpoints": [...] }`, so this is typed as a
named object with an `endpoints` array property (NOT a top-level array — the
live handler wraps the list under `endpoints`, and the wire shape is
preserved byte-for-byte).

DownloadResponse.description:
`POST /models/download` success body. Two states share the `name` + `status`
discriminant: a `status = "done"` response carries `already_present: true`
(weights already on disk, nothing queued); a `status = "downloading"`
response omits `already_present`. `already_present` is emitted **only** in
the done state, exactly as the prior ad-hoc JSON did.

DownloadStatusResponse.description:
`GET /models/download/{name}` progress body. Two genuinely-disjoint wire
shapes → an untagged enum (utoipa emits a `oneOf`), so the schema can never
mix the `installed` flag with the progress byte-counters:

- [`Installed`](Self::Installed) — no live job drives progress: either the
  weights are on disk (`status = "done"`, `installed = true`) or nothing is
  happening (`status = "idle"`, `installed = false`).
- [`Progress`](Self::Progress) — a live or terminal job's counters;
  `total_bytes` and `error` are emitted as `null` when unknown (never
  omitted), matching the prior ad-hoc JSON.

EndpointDescriptor.description: One entry in the [`DiscoveryResponse`] endpoint list.

ItemRunsResponse.description:
The `GET /items/{id}/runs` body: `{ "runs": RunRecord[] }`. A trivial
wrapper over the run list, reusing the already-typed `RunRecord` element so
the wire shape stays byte-identical to the prior `json!({ "runs": ... })`.

MeetingFull.description:
Full meeting detail: the `meeting_analyses` row. `result` (per-kind diarize
snapshot) and `speaker_names` (free-form id→name map) are dynamic and stay
`serde_json::Value`. Returned by `get_meeting`, `create_meeting` (201),
`patch_meeting`, and as each element of the meeting list.

result.description: Per-kind diarization result snapshot — shape varies, kept dynamic.

speaker_names.description: Free-form speaker id→name map — kept dynamic.

ModelEntry.description:
Schema mirror of [`registry::ModelListing`] (core crate: derives
`Serialize` but not `ToSchema`). It is referenced only via `value_type` for
the OpenAPI schema of the `models` array — the wire value is the real
`ModelListing`, whose snake_case keys and null-for-`None` optionals this
mirrors byte-for-byte, so serialization is unchanged.

ModelsListResponse.description:
`GET /models` success body: the active model name, whether its weights are
actually loaded, and the registry listing.

OpenAiModel.description: A single entry in the OpenAI-compatible model list (`GET /v1/models`).

id.description: Model identifier (the active ASR model name).

OpenAiModelList.description: The OpenAI-compatible model listing envelope returned by `GET /v1/models`.

data.description: The listed models (the single active ASR model).

OpenAiTranscription.description:
The default (`response_format=json`) transcription/translation body:
`{ "text": string }`. Non-default formats (`text`/`srt`/`vtt`/`verbose_json`)
are format-dependent and not schematized — see the operation descriptions.

PollError.description:
Terminal-error detail spliced into a poll response only when the job
failed. Omitted entirely for pending/running/done/cancelled jobs.

PollResponse.description:
Job-status body returned by `GET /transcribe/meeting/{id}`. `result` is the
per-kind analysis snapshot — `null` until the job is `done` — and is always
present. `error` is present only for a failed job.

stage.description: Current pipeline stage (asr | diarize | complete | failed | …).

RunAccepted.description:
The 202 run-accepted descriptor shared by every stage launcher: the opened
run's id and the poll URL for its job-status contract (`GET /runs/{id}`).
Wire shape: `{ "run_id": string, "status_url": string }` — field names map
1:1 to the JSON keys, so no `#[serde(rename)]` is needed.

RunKind.description:
The operation a run records. The `runs` table is the eventual superset of
all three kinds; this change writes only `Diarize` (the meeting pipeline).

RunOrigin.description:
Provenance of a run in the item listing (unify-run-ledger). `Stage` is a
real row in the `runs` ledger; `Capture` and `Legacy` are read-only runs
synthesized at list time from a session's `finals` / legacy `action_runs`,
so a read surface sees one unified history without reconciling the storage
split. Synthesized runs are never re-runnable.

RunRecord.description:
The job-status contract JSON returned by `GET /runs/{id}`. `params` is
stored in the table (D1) but deliberately NOT part of the status contract,
so it does not appear here.

origin.description:
Provenance (unify-run-ledger): `stage` for a real ledger row,
`capture`/`legacy` for a run synthesized at list time. Ledger reads are
always `stage`; the synthesizers set the other two.

result.description:
The run's immutable result snapshot (stage-run-endpoints, D8), parsed
from `result_json`. Serialized as `result`: null when the run has no
snapshot. Additive to the job-status contract.

RunStatus.description: The five lifecycle states every run reports (job-status contract).

SessionActionRun.description: One legacy `action_runs` row surfaced inside a session detail.

SessionFinal.description:
One finalized transcript segment inside a session (a `finals` row). Also the
standalone `201` body of `POST /v1/sessions/{id}/finals`, whose shape is
exactly this row.

SessionFull.description:
Full session detail: the `sessions` row plus its `finals` and legacy
`action_runs`. Returned by `get_session`, `create_session` (201),
`patch_session`, and as each element of the session list.

SetActiveResponse.description:
`POST /models/active` success body. `load_time_ms` is present **only** when
a load actually happened (`swapped = true`); it is omitted on the no-op path
(`swapped = false`, the model was already active and loaded). The key is
snake_case on the wire, matching the prior ad-hoc JSON.

quant.description: Quantization label; `null` when the format carries none.

load_time_ms.description: Model load time; `null` until weights are loaded.

loaded.description: false on a fresh install until `POST /models/active` loads weights.

StatusResponse.description: Success body of `GET /status` — engine health + active-model snapshot.

job_id.description: Opaque job id — poll `status_url` for progress.

status_url.description: Relative URL of the poll endpoint for this job.

TranscribeResponse.description:
Success body of `POST /transcribe`. Two wire shapes descend from one struct:
the empty/filtered case serializes to exactly `{ "text": "" }` (language and
segments omitted via `skip_serializing_if`), and the kept case adds
`language` and `segments`. `segments` is kept as `Vec<serde_json::Value>`
because the element (`whisper_wrap_core::asr::Segment`) implements
`Serialize` but not `ToSchema` and lives in the core crate — typing it here
would require touching core, so the shape is preserved without over-typing.

language.description: Detected language — omitted in the empty/filtered case.

segments.description: Timed segments — omitted in the empty/filtered case.
